diagram.title: System Block Diagram
diagram.noProject: No project loaded. Please select a project from the home page.
diagram.placeholder: Block diagram will be displayed after loading a project

coverage.title: Test Coverage
coverage.noProject: No project loaded. Please select a project from the home page.
coverage.getStartedDesc: Run tests and automatically collect coverage. Test framework is auto-detected.
coverage.noTestsFound: No test files found. All modules treated as 0% coverage.
coverage.noTestsFoundDesc: No test files or test framework detected in this project. All modules are at 0% coverage. The following modules need tests:
coverage.noReportGenerated: Tests completed but no coverage report was generated.
coverage.loadProjectFirst: Please open a project from the home page first.
coverage.customCommandHint: If auto-detection fails, enter the test command manually.
coverage.loadExistingHint: Load a pre-generated coverage report from CI/CD.
coverage.noMatchingFiles: No files match the current filter.

onboarding.step1Desc: Select a local project directory to analyze source code and visualize the system structure as a block diagram. Supports TypeScript, Python, Go, Rust, and C/C++.
onboarding.step2Desc: Load a test coverage report to overlay coverage information on the block diagram. Instantly see which modules need more testing.
onboarding.step3Desc: Analyze coverage gaps and generate a prioritized feedback file. Deploy it to your project and iterate: add tests, re-verify, improve.

help.title: User Guide
help.overviewDesc: Test Insight Hub is an independent test audit tool for AI-driven development. It visualizes project structure, auto-runs tests to collect coverage, analyzes test quality, and generates improvement feedback. It verifies code and tests written by project-side AI from an independent perspective.
help.step1Desc: Click 'Open Project' on the Home page and select the root directory of your target project.
help.step1Item1: Source code is analyzed automatically — module structure, dependencies, and functions are extracted
help.step1Item2: Results appear in the Block Diagram page (click 'Diagram' in the sidebar)
help.step2Desc: Click 'Run Tests & Get Coverage' on the Coverage page. The test framework (Vitest, Jest, pytest, go test, etc.) is auto-detected.
help.step2Item1: Test execution progress is shown in real-time in the log panel
help.step2Item2: File-level coverage percentages are listed after completion
help.step2Item3: Block diagram is color-coded by coverage (green: 80%+, yellow: 50%+, red: below 50%)
help.step3Desc: Check the Block Diagram page for an overview. Red modules need the most attention.
help.step3Item1: Click a module to open the detail panel on the right
help.step3Item2: View file-level coverage, untested functions, and associated tests
help.step3Item3: Navigate directly to feedback generation from the detail panel
help.step4Desc: Set a coverage threshold on the Feedback page and click 'Generate Feedback'. Gaps are detected with Line/Branch/Function coverage, test quality, and priority. Click 'Generate feedback for this module' from the block diagram to generate feedback for a specific module only.
help.step4Item1: Priority is calculated from coverage gap, code complexity, and change frequency
help.step4Item2: Required test scenarios (normal, error, boundary) are suggested for each file
help.step4Item3: Test quality analysis also identifies insufficient assertions in existing tests
help.step5Title: Step 5: Pass Feedback to Project-side AI
help.step5Desc: Pass the feedback to your project-side AI (Claude Code, etc.) to improve tests. Two methods are available.
help.step5Item1: 'Copy' button: Copies feedback content and prompt together to clipboard. Just paste it into Claude Code chat — the test improvement instruction is included
help.step5Item2: 'Deploy to Project' button: Outputs feedback JSON to .test-insight/feedback.json. Then instruct Claude Code in chat: 'Read .test-insight/feedback.json and add/improve tests for the indicated files'
help.step6Desc: After tests are improved, run coverage again to check the improvement.
help.step6Item1: Feedback History page shows comparison with previous runs and improvement rate
help.step6Item2: Repeat Steps 2-6 to progressively improve coverage
